data.narrative:
Evozyne is a Series B biotech company founded by Andrew Ferguson, Rama Ranganathan, and Jeff Aronin, having raised $144M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, spanning valuations from $600M to $3.3B at the Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $144M total raised proxy, yielding $274M. Peer-median anchoring across five same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—produces a median of $810M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Evozyne's revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S ratio across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is 6.6x but remains uninformative without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $233M to $931M, with a median of $542M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around the target's proprietary progress, clinical readiness, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set. The stage-progression floor suggests downside risk; peer-median positioning is materially higher. Investor conviction on underlying science and near-term catalysts would be required to narrow this range.
